locked_parameters.observable distribution: P over (Y,A,X), with A in \mathcal A finite and Y in [y_L,y_U]
locked_parameters.parameter \\theta: \theta(Q)=\operatorname{CVaR}_{\tau}\{Y_Q(\pi)\}, equivalently the sharp MSM-CVaR lower welfare endpoint \underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P) under the lower-is-better convention
locked_parameters.identifying restrictions: \mathcal R_{\mathrm{MSM}}, consisting of consistency, overlap on the support of \pi, and the odds-ratio marginal sensitivity bound indexed by \Gamma
locked_parameters.bounding functional: (\theta_L(P),\theta_U(P)) defined in Section~\ref{sec:functional}, with \theta_U(P)=\underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P) given by the nested CVaR bounding functional \mathsf B_{\mathrm{CVaR}}; sharpness verdict pending the downstream conjecture stages

named_assumptions.label: ass:iid-overlap
named_assumptions.statement: The observations O_1,\ldots,O_n are iid from P. For each a\in\mathcal A, the nominal propensity score e_a(X)=P(A=a\mid X) satisfies 0<\epsilon\leq e_a(X)\leq 1-\epsilon almost surely, and \pi(a\mid X)>0 implies e_a(X)>0 almost surely.

named_assumptions.label: ass:consistency
named_assumptions.statement: For every action a\in\mathcal A, the factual outcome satisfies Y=Y(A) almost surely, and the policy draw A^\pi used to define Y(\pi) is conditionally independent of the factual treatment assignment given X.

named_assumptions.label: ass:msm
named_assumptions.statement: There exists a latent variable U such that, for every a\in\mathcal A and every (x,u) in the support of (X,U), \Gamma^{-1}\leq \frac{P(A=a\mid X=x)/(1-P(A=a\mid X=x))}{P(A=a\mid X=x,U=u)/(1-P(A=a\mid X=x,U=u))}\leq \Gamma.

named_assumptions.label: ass:tail
named_assumptions.statement: The conditional law of Y\mid A=a,X=x is supported on the common compact interval [y_L,y_U] for every (a,x). For each \eta\in[y_L,y_U], the generated loss (Y-\eta)_+ has finite conditional mean in every (a,x) cell, and the conditional law of Y\mid A=a,X=x has no degeneracy at the active truncation thresholds used by the least-favorable MSM tilt.

named_assumptions.label: ass:unique-eta
named_assumptions.statement: The criterion \eta\mapsto \mathcal C_\eta(P,\pi) defined in Section~\ref{sec:functional} has a unique minimizer \eta_{\tau,\Gamma}(P,\pi) in the interior of \mathcal I=[y_L,y_U], and its left and right derivatives straddle zero at that point.

named_assumptions.label: ass:nuisance
named_assumptions.statement: The first-stage estimators of the propensity scores, active truncation thresholds, and tilted truncated-loss regressions are obtained on auxiliary folds and satisfy the standard product-rate condition under which products of first-stage L_2(P) errors are o_p(n^{-1/2}).

conjectures.label: nested-endpoint

method_exemplar.carried_over: Mirror the sharp MSM policy-value setup and replace the linear mean target with the eta-indexed truncated-loss family, using Rockafellar-Uryasev CVaR minimization as the outer layer.

conjectures.label: noncommutation
conjectures.statement:
(NOT YET PROVED.)  Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:iid-overlap}--\ref{ass:tail},
\[
\Delta_{\mathrm{nc}}(\pi;P)=
W_{\tau,\Gamma}^{\mathrm{band}}(\pi;P)
-\underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)\geq 0.
\]
Moreover, there is a two-action, three-outcome, one-covariate-cell class satisfying strict overlap and \(1<\Gamma<\infty\) on which \(\Delta_{\mathrm{nc}}(\pi;P)>0\).  Equality holds when the same monotone least-favorable MSM tilt is simultaneously optimal for all \(\eta\) in the active subgradient set of \(\mathcal C_\eta(P,\pi)\).

method_exemplar.carried_over: Use the direct-sharp versus indirect-band contrast as the template for showing that a coordinatewise envelope can overstate a nonlinear rank-dependent policy functional.

conjectures.label: onestep-limit
conjectures.statement:
(NOT YET PROVED.)  Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:iid-overlap}--\ref{ass:nuisance}, the estimator in Definition~\ref{def:onestep} is asymptotically linear:
\[
\sqrt n\{\hat W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi)-\underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)\}
=
\frac{1}{\sqrt n}\sum_{i=1}^n
\varphi_{\tau,\Gamma,\pi}(O_i)+o_p(1),
\qquad
\E[\varphi_{\tau,\Gamma,\pi}(O)]=0.
\]
The variance of \(\varphi_{\tau,\Gamma,\pi}\) is consistently estimated by the fold-stacked empirical variance of \(\hat\eta+\tau^{-1}\hat\phi_{\hat\eta,\pi}-\hat W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi)\).

method_exemplar.carried_over: Adapt the orthogonal sharp-endpoint score to the generated truncated-loss family and pair the resulting endpoint CLT with Imbens-Manski interval calibration.

writing_exemplar.mirrored_intro_arc: observational policy learning -> hidden confounding -> sharp MSM mean-value bounds -> unresolved tail welfare

substrate_survey.summary: Surveyed the requested partial-ID substrate names with Lean search tools before drafting. No direct primitive was reused in the shared setup because the existing indexed partial-ID names are Manski/ATE-oriented, while this note's shared layer is a generic fixed-policy MSM-CVaR functional over (Y,A,X).

queries.name: POManskiIVSystem
queries.reason: The setup here is not an IV-style Manski system; it is a generic MSM policy-value functional.

queries.name: manski_bounds_ATE
queries.reason: ATE endpoint primitives sit at a different target than the policy-level CVaR endpoint.

queries.name: Manski.MTR
queries.reason: This theorem instance is driven by marginal sensitivity rather than MTR/MTS/MIV shape restrictions.

queries.name: PartialID.Basic
queries.reason: The generic math-note setup is stated directly at the observable/full-data-law level without committing to a particular in-repo wrapper.

queries.name: POSystem partial identification
queries.reason: The broad search did not return a reusable fixed-policy MSM-CVaR primitive, so the setup remains abstraction-first.
